summary: Created comprehensive anti-patterns reference document cataloging common AI design clichés across 6 categories: color, layout, typography, imagery, animation, and copy. Each anti-pattern includes description, why it's problematic, and what to do instead. Added practical taste test checklist for self-review.
learnings: Anti-patterns reference needed specific examples (hex codes, exact phrases) to be useful
Taste test checklist provides quick validation tool beyond detailed reference
Structure of what/why/instead makes the document actionable rather than just informative
Focus on AI design 'tells' helps differentiate from generic design advice
notes: Document structured for quick scanning and practical application. Emphasized specific, recognizable AI design patterns rather than general design principles. Taste test section provides rapid validation workflow.
